title: Storage autodetect mount program
body: This is a pull request including https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/1165 as requested by @TomSweeneyRedHat in https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/1165#issuecomment-1069698116

comments.body:
I really don't know what's going wrong here:
```
go: downloading github.com/openshift/imagebuilder v1.2.2
verifying github.com/openshift/imagebuilder@v1.2.2: checksum mismatch
	downloaded: h1:++jWWMkTVJKP2MIjTPaTk2MqwWIOYYlDaQbZyLlLBh0=
	go.sum:     h1:eXTZk6YxykHKq1vtKTiZVyd2aJVP3496AWJ9U09wXrk=
```
The `go.sum` was generated on my machine with `make vendor`.
Recommend using `make vendor-in-container` - we've have issues with this previously from Go version mismatches

> Recommend using `make vendor-in-container` - we've have issues with this previously from Go version mismatches

Thanks! I've given that a try, let's see if that fixes the issue.

@dcermak Looks like a possible race condition error.....


This looks like a possible bug in #13404. @flouthoc PTAL.
I have rebased my PR on the latest main branch and the tests are now green.
Please rebase off of the merged PR in containers/storage and then we can merge this PR.
@rhatdan Done!
@rhatdan Could this be backported to the 3.4 branch as well please?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *dcermak*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> /approve LGTM @dcermak doubtful backporing to 3.4. Is this needed for RHEL?

We've hit this bug in openSUSE Leap 15.3 and SLE 15 SP3, but given that RHEL 8 ships podman 3.4 and kernel 4.18, you're most likely going to hit this bug there as well. So yes, I think it is needed.

We were considering a v3.4.5 at some point in the semi-near future (this week, maybe?) so while this probably can't go in RHEL it can go in the upstream v3.4 branch for that release.

/cherry-pick v3.4

@mheon: once the present PR merges, I will cherry-pick it on top of v3.4 in a new PR and assign it to you.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13536#issuecomment-1073877759):

>We were considering a v3.4.5 at some point in the semi-near future (this week, maybe?) so while this probably can't go in RHEL it can go in the upstream v3.4 branch for that release.
>
>/cherry-pick v3.4


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

>Could it be some other test which overlaps and cause this rare flake

System tests run sequentially. There is no parallelism. I haven't tried to reproduce this flake, all I meant to do was signal that it is alarming to have this test fail.
@edsantiago Indeed it is alarming this should never fail, thanks for notifying me. I'll keep checking and will update if I find anything, will try to repro this in different env.
@rhatdan Can we merge this? The CI is green and you've approved it, but the tide check says that it needs a lgtm label, maybe that's missing?
/lgtm

Matt Heon ***@***.***> writes:

> We were considering a v3.4.5 at some point in the semi-near future (this week, maybe?) so while this probably can't go in RHEL it can go in the upstream v3.4 branch for that release.

Thanks! Would you CC me on that PR as well, so that I can test it on
openSUSE Leap as well?
3.4 is presently on 3.4.7 - that release was made on April 20th. That is the last release out of the 3.4 series; everything from here on is going to be on 4.x branches.

@mheon sorry for the comment https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13536#issuecomment-1274313807, I have sent that one **months** ago and it just appeared a few hours ago :thinking:. Some mail queue got delayed by quite a bit… That comment is obsolete now anyway, as I have backported this PR back in March.
Ack, no problem. My Github emails usually just don't appear at all when I use email, so I am not surprised.
